rationale.root_cause: It identifies the unsafe copy at program.c:10 using strcpy on argv data and explains that the attacker-controlled argument can exceed buf[256], causing a stack buffer overflow. It explicitly names the issue as a stack buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: It proposes strncpy with explicit null-termination but does not reject overlong input before copying as required. No length check to fail early is included.
rationale.global_fix: It recommends replacing raw, unbounded string handling with bounded interfaces and enforcing input-length checks at the API boundary, which removes unbounded copies from the data path. This matches the criterion to use bounded string APIs or centralize validation.
